summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn delivered a passionate message titled 'Burn It Down,' framing it as a battle cry for personal and spiritual renewal. He opened by recounting his own conversion story, where he literally burned items from his past life to symbolize a point of no return with God. Using the examples of military strategies to burn bridges and boats, he challenged the congregation to identify what they need to 'burn down' in their own lives. The core of the sermon was built on the story of King Josiah from 2 Kings, who led a national revival by removing idolatry from the temple. Pastor Travis outlined a three-step process for revival: Repent, Remove, and Renew. He emphasized that true repentance involves both confession and forsaking sin, leading to God's forgiveness and purification. He connected this to the need to remove anything from our lives—our modern 'temples'—that does not honor God, whether it's lust, greed, or other influences. Finally, he called for a renewal of commitment to Christ, reminding everyone that God's grace is sufficient to restore anyone, no matter how far they have wandered. The message concluded with a powerful altar call for salvation and recommitment, linking personal repentance to the collective revival the church and world need.
mainPoints: True revival begins with total repentance—confessing and forsaking our sins, which opens the door to God's forgiveness and purification.
We must remove anything from our lives (our 'temples') that does not honor God, taking decisive action to 'burn down' past influences and sinful practices.
Every believer needs to renew their covenant and commitment to follow Jesus wholeheartedly, trusting in His grace to restore and bless a repentant heart.

versesMentioned.text: Many of those who believed now came and openly confessed what they had done. A number who had practiced sorcery brought their scrolls together and burned them publicly. When they calculated the value of the scrolls, the total came to fifty thousand drachmas. In this way the word of the Lord spread widely and grew in power.
But if I say, 'I will not mention his word or speak anymore in his name,' his word is in my heart like a fire, a fire shut up in my bones. I am weary of holding it in; indeed, I cannot.

Josiah was eight years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem thirty-one years. His mother’s name was Jedidah daughter of Adaiah; she was from Bozkath. He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD and followed completely the ways of his father David, not turning aside to the right or to the left.
'These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me.'

The king ordered Hilkiah the high priest, the priests next in rank and the doorkeepers to remove from the temple of the LORD all the articles made for Baal and Asherah and all the starry hosts. He burned them outside Jerusalem in the fields of the Kidron Valley and took the ashes to Bethel.
Neither before nor after Josiah was there a king like him who turned to the LORD as he did—with all his heart and with all his soul and with all his strength, in accordance with all the Law of Moses.
Whoever conceals their sins does not prosper, but the one who confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.
Peter replied, 'Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. And you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.'
Those who accepted his message were baptized, and about three thousand were added to their number that day.

if my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land.

Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? You are not your own; you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.
Therefore, get rid of all moral filth and the evil that is so prevalent and humbly accept the word planted in you, which can save you.

Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal to win the prize for which God has called me heavenward in Christ Jesus.

Yet I hold this against you: You have forsaken the love you had at first. Consider how far you have fallen! Repent and do the things you did at first. If you do not repent, I will come to you and remove your lampstand from its place.

Have mercy on me, O God, according to your unfailing love; according to your great compassion blot out my transgressions. Wash away all my iniquity and cleanse me from my sin... Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me. Do not cast me from your presence or take your Holy Spirit from me.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: 1 John 1:9 (NIV) - If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.
Monday.action: Take a quiet moment to confess one specific area where you need God's forgiveness. Write it down, then tear up or safely destroy the paper as a symbol of release.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for your promise to forgive and purify me. I confess my need for you and receive your cleansing grace today.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 28:13 (NIV) - Whoever conceals their sins does not prosper, but the one who confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
Tuesday.focus: The power of confession and renunciation.
Tuesday.action: Identify one habit or influence you need to 'renounce.' Take a practical step to limit its access in your life today (e.g., adjust a social media setting, avoid a certain place).
Tuesday.prayer: God, give me the courage to not just confess but to renounce what holds me back. I choose your mercy over my hidden sin.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 6:19-20 (NIV) - ...your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit... Therefore honor God with your bodies.
Wednesday.focus: Honoring God with our physical lives.
Wednesday.action: Evaluate one aspect of how you treat your body (diet, rest, what you watch/listen to). Make one positive choice today that honors God as your body's caretaker.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, you dwell in me. Help me to see my body as your temple and make choices today that bring you honor.

Thursday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead, I press on toward the goal...
Thursday.action: Reflect on a past failure or hurt. Consciously decide to 'forget what is behind' by not dwelling on it today. Instead, write down one goal you are straining toward in Christ.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to leave my past with you. Empower me to press on toward the future you have for me, fueled by your grace.

Friday.scripture: 2 Kings 23:25 (NIV) - Neither before nor after Josiah was there a king like him who turned to the LORD as he did—with all his heart...
Friday.action: Renew your personal covenant with God. Speak or write a simple prayer of recommitment, offering your heart, soul, and strength to Him anew.
Friday.prayer: Lord, like Josiah, I turn to you with all my heart, soul, and strength. I renew my commitment to follow you completely. Reignite your fire in me.

Saturday.scripture: Acts 19:19-20 (NIV) - ...they burned them publicly... In this way the word of the Lord spread widely and grew in power.
Saturday.action: Prepare your heart for corporate worship. Consider sharing a testimony of God's work in your life this week with one trusted person, encouraging them in their faith.
Saturday.prayer: God, as your word spread through the testimony of believers, use my story for your glory. Let my life point others to your powerful, life-changing love.
